text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE IMAGING — POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [on file]
AGE: 49 years
SEX: Female
INDICATION: Surveillance imaging 18 months following matched unrelated donor allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation for AML with myelodysplasia-related changes, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk disease. Prior history of mild chronic GVHD (skin and oral, now resolved off immunosuppression). History of pre-transplant splenomegaly (14.2 cm at diagnosis, normalized at 6-month restaging). Evaluating for evidence of disease recurrence or late transplant-related complications.
MODALITY: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired with intravenous contrast (iohexol 350 mg I/mL, 100 mL); portal venous phase for abdomen/pelvis; arterial-to-portal phase for chest. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats generated.
COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, day +180 post-alloHSCT (approximately 6 months prior). PET-CT pre-transplant restaging (approximately 18 months prior). CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at AML diagnosis (approximately 22 months prior).

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well-aerated bilaterally with no focal areas of consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or airspace disease. No pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of detection on this non-PET modality. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level without endobronchial lesion. There is no bronchiectasis and no mosaic attenuation pattern to suggest air trapping or obliterative bronchiolitis. The lung parenchyma appears normal in attenuation and architecture throughout both upper, middle, and lower lobes. No cavitary lesions or cystic changes. No tree-in-bud opacities or peribronchiolar nodularity to suggest infectious bronchiolitis.

Pleura and Pericardium: No pleural effusions, bilateral. No pleural thickening or calcification. No pericardial effusion. The pericardium appears thin and unremarkable.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal contour is normal. The thymus appears involuted consistent with age and prior systemic chemotherapy and conditioning regimen. No anterior mediastinal mass. No prevascular, pretracheal, paratracheal, subcarinal, or aortopulmonary window lymph nodes meeting size criteria for enlargement (all visible nodes measure well under 1 cm in short-axis diameter). The hila are symmetric and unremarkable without lymphadenopathy. No mass lesion.

Heart and Great Vessels: Cardiac chambers appear normal in size. No intracardiac filling defect. The aorta is normal in caliber throughout the thoracic course. The pulmonary arteries are opacified without filling defect to suggest acute pulmonary embolism. No evidence of central pulmonary arterial dilation.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: No chest wall masses, subcutaneous lesions, or soft tissue collections. Visualized bony structures of the thoracic cage including the ribs, sternum, clavicles, and thoracic spine show no focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No pathologic fracture.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size (craniocaudal span approximately 14.3 cm in the midclavicular line) and demonstrates homogeneous parenchymal enhancement without focal lesion. No hypodense or hyperdense mass identified. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation. The hepatic vasculature — portal vein, hepatic veins, and inferior vena cava at the hepatic level — is patent and without thrombosis. No periportal edema or evidence of sinusoidal obstruction. No ascites in the perihepatic space.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: The gallbladder is normal in size, without wall thickening, gallstones, or pericholecystic fluid. The common bile duct measures 3 mm at the hepatic hilum and is not dilated. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size, measuring approximately 9.4 cm in craniocaudal dimension, representing continued resolution of the splenomegaly that measured 14.2 cm at initial AML diagnosis and was 9.8 cm at the 6-month post-transplant imaging. Splenic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal lesion, infarct, or accessory splenic tissue. No perisplenic fluid. This finding is stable and reassuring compared to the prior study.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size, contour, and enhancement pattern throughout the head, uncinate process, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic fat stranding or fluid collection. No mass lesion.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and morphology with symmetrical and prompt parenchymal enhancement bilaterally. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi identified. No renal mass or cystic lesion of concern. No perinephric fluid collections. The bilateral ureters are not dilated to the extent visualized.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in size and configuration. No adrenal nodule or mass.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The visualized segments of the esophagus, stomach, and small and large bowel appear unremarkable. No bowel wall thickening, dilatation, or mass. No pneumoperitoneum or free intraperitoneal air. No peritoneal nodularity or omental thickening to suggest peritoneal infiltration.

Abdominal Lymph Nodes: No enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the para-aortic, portocaval, gastrohepatic, or mesenteric distributions. Multiple small sub-centimeter nodes are visible in the para-aortic region, all measuring under 8 mm in short axis, and are within normal limits. There is no lymphadenopathy to suggest extramedullary AML (myeloid sarcoma/chloroma) at any abdominal site.

Ascites and Peritoneum: No free abdominal fluid. Peritoneum is smooth and unremarkable.

Abdominal Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric arteries are patent. The portal and superior mesenteric veins are patent without thrombus.

PELVIS:

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic lymph nodes meeting size criteria for pathologic enlargement. Bilateral iliac and inguinal regions show only small, morphologically normal-appearing lymph nodes.

Pelvic Organs: The uterus appears normal in size and morphology. Bilateral ovaries are not individually identified, which is within normal limits given age and hormonal/systemic treatment history. No pelvic free fluid. Urinary bladder is decompressed at the time of imaging but appears without intraluminal mass or wall irregularity on the available views.

Bowel: No pelvic bowel abnormality. The rectosigmoid colon is unremarkable.

Bony Pelvis and Visible Spine: The bones of the pelvis — iliac wings, sacrum, pubic rami, bilateral femoral heads — are without focal lytic or sclerotic lesion. No cortical destruction or periosteal reaction. The visualized lumbar and sacral spine demonstrates no suspicious osseous lesion. No compression deformity. The visualized portions of the proximal femora are unremarkable.

Soft Tissues: No soft tissue mass in the pelvis. No hematoma or fluid collection.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No imaging evidence of AML relapse or extramedullary disease (myeloid sarcoma/chloroma). No hepatic, splenic, nodal, or osseous lesions to raise concern for hematologic malignancy recurrence.

2. Normal spleen size at approximately 9.4 cm, with continued normalization since initial presentation. Resolution of the splenomegaly noted at the time of AML diagnosis is maintained and stable compared to the 6-month post-transplant study.

3. The lungs are clear bilaterally. No ground-glass opacity, mosaic attenuation, or bronchiectasis to suggest bronchiolitis obliterans or chronic pulmonary GVHD. No pulmonary infection or consolidation.

4. No thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

5. No pleural or pericardial effusion.

6. No osseous lesions to suggest leukemic infiltration or treatment-related osteonecrosis in the visualized skeleton.

7. Overall appearance is stable compared to prior restaging CT at 6 months post-transplant, consistent with ongoing complete remission without imaging evidence of disease recurrence or significant late post-transplant complication.

Radiology cancer status: no cancer identified on imaging.

Signed: [Attending Radiologist, Hematologic Malignancy/Body Imaging Division]
